_comment: One file per procedure_code. Distilled from the matching pair in PM JAY/Standard Treatment Guideline/master/<condition>/ : _Medco.pdf (clinical + mandatory docs) + _PPD.CPD.pdf (PPD/CPD processing checklists). Filename = procedure_code. This is what the Documentation Checker, Query Predictor, and Medical Justification agents read AFTER the picker has chosen the code.
clinical_indications: Any acute onset illness with typical skin features (urticarial rash or erythema/flushing, and/or angioedema), involvement of respiratory and/or cardiovascular and/or persistent severe gastrointestinal symptoms
Any acute onset of hypotension or bronchospasm or upper airway obstruction where anaphylaxis is considered possible, even if typical skin features are not present

preauth.label: Clinical Notes including history (Drug Hx, IV, Sub cutaneous TM, Oral), physical examination, evaluation findings, indications for the procedure, and planned line of treatment
Complete Blood Count (CBC)/ allergen-specific immunoglobulin E (IgE) report

ppd_preauth.q: Were the Clinical Notes including history (Drug Hx, IV, Sub cutaneous TM, Oral), physical examination, evaluation findings, indications for the procedure, and planned line of treatment submitted?
Was the Complete Blood Count (CBC)/ allergen-specific immunoglobulin E (IgE) report submitted?
Are the clinical notes and Laboratory diagnostic reports are indicative of the procedure?

cpd_claim.q: Was the detailed Indoor case papers with all the daily vital and treatment details submitted?
Was the post treatment CBC report submitted?
Was the detailed discharge summary with the follow up date submitted?

common_queries: Clinical notes or laboratory diagnostic reports are not indicative of Anaphylaxis
Clinical Notes including history, physical examination, evaluation findings, indications, and planned line of treatment are missing or incomplete at pre-authorization
Complete Blood Count (CBC) or allergen-specific immunoglobulin E (IgE) report is missing at pre-authorization
Detailed Indoor case papers with daily vital and treatment details are missing or incomplete at claim submission
Post treatment CBC report is missing at claim submission
Detailed Discharge Summary with follow-up date is missing or incomplete at claim submission

additional_information.min_doctor_qualification_desirable: MD / DNB General medicine, MD / DNB Pediatric Medicine/Emergency Medicine
clinical_key_pointers: Mild or moderate reactions: Swelling of lips, face, eyes
Mild or moderate reactions: Hives or welts
Mild or moderate reactions: Tingling mouth
Mild or moderate reactions: Abdominal pain,
Respiratory/chest features (most common in children): Anxiety, Angioedema
Respiratory/chest features (most common in children): Breathing difficulty
Respiratory/chest features (most common in children): Hypotension (Low BP)
Respiratory/chest features (most common in children): Sever/life threatening anaphylaxis
Respiratory/chest features (most common in children): Diarrhoea
Respiratory/chest features (most common in children): Abdominal cramps
Respiratory/chest features (most common in children): Nausea / Vomiting
Respiratory/chest features (most common in children): Sneezing or Rhinorrhea
Sever anaphylaxis: Cardiorespiratory, Breathing difficulty wheezing, Hemodynamic instability, compromise hypotension, cardiovascular collapse.
